places.title: Hanoi Jeep Tour: Food, Culture and Fun by Vietnam Army Jeep
places.description: Experience the sights, sounds, and smells of Hanoi in a unique way as you explore the city in a Soviet-era GAZ-69 jeep. See a mix of city highlights and hidden gems in the capital of Vietnam and learn about the country’s history from your guide. Choose from morning, afternoon, or evening tours and enjoy a delicious street food meal.

places.title: Hanoi Free Tour Guides
places.description: Hanoi Free Tour Guides is a social non-profit organization founded and run by a group of students and ex-students.Hanoi Free Tour Guides is aimed at promoting and publicly introducing the images of Vietnam, its country and people to foreigners, improving cross-cultural communication and building up a bridge for the enhancement of international relations for the betterment of the society. HFTGs' services are now available in English, Chinese, French, Russian, Korean Japanese, German, Spanish and Italian. Whether you're interested in historical landmarks, local cuisine, or hidden gems off the beaten path, your tour with us shall be an unforgettable memory. Thanks to its great contributions to the public, HFTGs is now well-known as Hanoi Little Ambassadors.

places.title: Hanoi City Tour Highlight, Train Street Combine visit Countryside
places.description: Instead of spending ages trapped in the Hanoi traffic in a crowded tour bus or minivan, see a number of attractions from the back of a nimble Vespa scooter on this brisk half-day tour. Explore the remnants of French colonialism in the French Quarter, drive around natural landmarks like the West Lake and Red River Island, see remarkable historical highlights like the Long Bien Bridge, Phu Tay Ho Temple and the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum.

places.title: Small Group Hanoi Jeep Tour All Inclusive
places.description: This tour combines sightseeing, cultural immersion, and food exploration, making it an unforgettable experience!

highlighted_review.text: It was a nice experience and guide for the main attractions in Hanoi and

places.title: Full-Day Ninh Binh Highlights Tour from Hanoi
places.description: Skip the hassle of piecing together boats and bike rentals and savor Ninh Binh’s highlights on this guided small-group tour. Visit Hoa Lu, once Vietnam's capital, explore Mua Cave and mountain views, ride a boat through Tam Coc caves, and cycle to Bich Dong Pagoda. Enjoy an authentic Vietnamese lunch and hotel pickup and drop-off by limousine minivan.

places.title: West Lake
places.description: The largest lake n Hanoi is a popular spot for gathering, surrounded by many shops, restaurants, gardens, and hotels. It features the Trấn Quốc Pagoda, Vietnamese for "Stabilizing the Nation."

places.title: Incense Village, Conical Hat, Lacquer Art, SMALL GROUP From Hanoi
places.description: Explore the rich traditions of Vietnamese handicrafts on this immersive half-day tour from Hanoi. Begin with a pickup in the Old Quarter and visit the renowned Chuong village, famous for its conical hat-making, where you’ll meet local artisans and observe the intricate craft. Next, head to Quang Phu Cau village, known for its century-long incense production, where you can see the colorful incense-making process firsthand. This tour encompasses cultural insights and artisan techniques, offering a unique glimpse into Vietnamese life.  - Duration: Approximately 6 hours with morning and afternoon options - Visit two traditional villages: Conical Hat and Incense Craft - Engage with local artisans and learn about their crafts - Optional drop-off at Hanoi Train Street after the tour

highlighted_review.text: We got a full day trip visiting 3 attractions in hanoi outskirts.

places.title: Vietnam Museum of Ethnology
places.description: Vietnam Museum of Ethnology is a unit under Vietnam Academy of Scocial Sciences. Vietnam Museum of Ethnology is both a research centre and a public museum exhibiting the ethnic groups of Vietnam. The mission of the Museum is scientific research, collection, documentation, conservation, exhibition and preserving the cultural and historic patrimony of the nation's different ethnic groups. The museum also serves to guide research, conservation, and technology that are specific to the work of an ethnographic museum.

highlighted_review.text: For us, it was the highlight attraction of Hanoi.

places.title: Ninh Binh Full-Day Small Group of 9 Guided Tour from Hanoi
places.description: Combine three scenic attractions in one tour with this full-day small-group tour ( 9 travellers per group) to Ninh Binh: Mua Cave, Tam Coc, and Hoa Lu - ancient capital: - Go up to the top of mountain at Mua Cave to have overview of Ninh Binh and limestone moutain system. - Easy and fun cycling on flat ground, around rice fields next to limestone mountain (relax at restaurant if you can not bike)  - See local life in countryside of Vietnam. - Boating at Tam Coc, known as the Halong on land. - Visit Hoa Lu - the ancient capital - Includes all entrance fees, lunch, bottled water (3 bottles/per) - Gain more from your visit with a local guide to provide commentary

places.title: Hanoi Heritage Premium Daily Tour
places.description: Hanoi Capital is always of the top destinations in Vietnam for many foreign travelers. As one of the leading trend operators, Ha Noi Heritage Premium Daily Tours has organized the widest choice of luxury tours including must-see attractions such as Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum; Tran Quoc Pagoda; Vietnam Museum of Ethnology; Temple of Literature... We use luxury limousines transfer service, and own our private Luxury restaurant call "Mesdames Linh Cuisine" serving the best local specialties. Moreover, we establish and maintain strong rapport with our customers, prospects, and bussiness partners, providing the top-quality tour at value-for-money prices. Ha Noi Heritage Premium consistently emphasizes satisfying our guests and meeting our customers’expectations.

places.title: Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum
places.description: **This place is temporarily closed** Every year many thousands of respectful citizens and tourists visit the final resting place of the country’s most notable historic figure.

highlighted_review.text: If you’re in Hanoi this is a must but BE PREPARED!

places.title: Hanoi Opera House
places.description: Resembling the Opéra Palais Garnier, Hanoi Opera House, built in 10 years since 1911 by two French architects. The magnificent building became one of the top landmarks in Hanoi. The Opera House hosts cultural entertaining performances, bamboo cirque, contemporary dance, orchestra concerts and government events. Come to the front gate to check availability and ticket to a performance in this iconic building.

places.title: Hanoi City Tour: Private Half-Day Customized with Train Street
places.description: This private half-day tour of Hanoi is unique for its blend of iconic landmarks, cultural immersion, and local experiences. You'll visit the serene Tran Quoc Pagoda, the historic Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, and the thrilling Train Street, where a train passes inches from homes. Explore traditional craft streets in the Old Quarter, where artisans continue age-old trades, and end at the vibrant Dong Xuan Market, offering a taste of local life. The tour’s personalized nature allows for a deeper, more intimate connection with Hanoi’s rich heritage in a short amount of time.

highlighted_review.text: of Hanoi and life in Vietnam.

places.title: Hanoi Car
places.description: Today Hanoi Car Tours and Transportation are one of the leading tour operators and providing great services for Transfer, the cruise ships passengers in Ha Long Bay, Sapa, Eco-Trang An grottoes and all the other tours in Vietnam.

places.title: Private Guided Tour of Hanoi
places.description: Explore Hanoi’s Old Quarter on a private guided walking tour with a local student guide. Choose from a half-day or full-day tour. Get picked up at your hotel in the Old Quarter and head to the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, Temple of Literature, Hoa Lo Prison, Ancient House, and Hoan Kiem Lake.

highlighted_review.text: We had a very informative tour of several attractions in Hanoi in a short

places.title: Hanoi Highlights: Half-day City Tour
places.description: Explore Hanoi’s rich architecture and culture on a tour that fits all of the highlights into a half-day excursion perfect for busy travelers who have limited time in the city. While the bustling city can be overwhelming on your own, with a small-group tour you don’t have to worry about navigating the busy streets and can learn about the sites along the way from your guide.

highlighted_review.text: I had a good tour with my guide CRIS, he led us to many Hanoi attractions

places.title: Asiatica Travel
places.description: Asiatica Travel is a tour operator based in Vietnam, offering not just private day trips in Hanoi but also customized private journeys to Vietnam, Laos and Cambodia. Founded in 2001 by four local guides, Asiatica is now one of the biggest and most trustworthy local tour operators in Indochina, includes also Myanmar and Thailand. International tourism license No. 0513/LHQT

places.title: Long Bien Bridge
places.description: The Long Bien bridge across the Red River connects Hanoi with the main port of Haiphong. Designed by Gustave Eiffel, the bridge sustained significant damage during the Vietnam War. Only half of the bridge retains its original shape, but a rebuilding project supported by the French government is currently in progress to restore the bridge to its original appearance.

places.title: Local Market Tour and Private Cooking Class
places.description: Explore Hanoi’s food and culture scene with this private market tour followed by a hands-on cooking class. You’ll learn all about the fresh produce in the market, and travel around in a cyclo- the way the locals get around. You’ll then get back to your guide’s kitchen, where you’ll prepare a traditional three course meal before you get to eat it with your private group.

places.title: Hanoi City Half Day Private Tour: Hidden Corners & Train Street
places.description: With thousands of years of history and entrenched in a clash of cultures, this private half-day tour lets you explore Hanoi with a local guide. Travel from traditional pagodas to bustling markets and serene lakes, going beyond guidebooks into hidden corners. See Hanoi Old Quarter's 2,000-year history, snap photos of French colonial architecture, and experience the thrill of Train Street—a must-do when visiting Hanoi.

highlighted_review.text: We got to see so many attractions in Hanoi.

places.title: Hanoi City Tour : Hanoi Highlights and Hidden Gems
places.description: Experience the Best of Hanoi with the Half Day Hanoi City Tour to discover Hanoi Highlights & Hidden Gems. This exceptional Hanoi city tour provides an opportunity to delve into the historical city, immerse in its captivating culture, and uncover its hidden treasures.  By choosing our small group tour in a comfortable and luxurious minivan, you can enhance your experience by avoiding wasted time waiting for others and dealing with crowds. It’s also a great opportunity to meet new people and embark on a delightful journey together.  Discover the must-see sights in Hanoi, including the Ho Chi Minh Complex, Temple of Literature, French Quarter, Old Quarter, Long Bien Bridge, Westlake, and the famous Hanoi Train Street.  Uncover the lesser-known treasures of Hanoi by venturing through its labyrinth of narrow alleyways and immersing yourself in the daily lives of locals.  Embark on an authentic Hanoi experience that showcases the city’s unique blend of the good & the bad.

places.title: Private Crowd Free Hanoi: Morning Highlights of the City Experience
places.description: Follow in the footsteps of one of Vietnam’s most famous historic men, Ho Chi Minh ( first president of the Democratic Republic of Vietnam), as you explore the streets of Hanoi on this private tour. Pay a visit to the Temple of Literature and the notorious "Hanoi Hilton" (Hoa Loa Prison). Then, follow your guide on a walking tour of the Old-Quarter and enjoy the inside knowledge that comes from being led by a local.

places.title: Small Group Half-day Hanoi City Tour with Train Street Visit (4h)
places.description: Discover the best of Vietnam's capital as you embark on a half-day sightseeing tour of Hanoi in an air-conditioned van. Join a small group and get acquainted with the city's daily life, scenery, and culture. Visit several highlights including the Ancient House, Art Center, Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, Tran Quoc Pagoda, Hoa Lo Prison, and the famous train street.

places.title: Hanoi Happy Tours
places.description: Hanoi Happy Tours offers tours to every single corner of the historic and beautiful Hanoi with informative guides. We are proud of having a highly professional team with deep understanding of the area to bring out the best of the city. We are also eager to show you the very different spirit of Hanoi and its beauty as well as giving you the chance to truly immerse yourselves into local life.

places.title: Hanoi Old Quarter Street Food Tours
places.description: Any visitor to Hanoi is faced with a multitude of choices when it comes to Vietnamese cuisine. This walking tour with a foodie as your guide takes away the guesswork. Let an insider lead the way to some of the best food stands and eateries in Hanoi’s Old Quarter. You’ll observe local life while filling up on flavors that make Hanoi a culinary destination.

places.title: Imperial Citadel of Thang Long
places.description: Welcome to the Imperial Citadel of Thang Long – Hanoi! Situated at the heart of Hanoi, the Central Sector of the Imperial Citadel of Thang Long is an outstanding place of interest not only for the capital city but also for the country as a whole. The site is one of the ten special national heritage sites proclaimed by the Prime Minister in 2009 and was inscribed on the World Heritage List by UNESCO’s World Heritage Committee in 2010. Its Outstanding Universal Values are reflected in its historical longevity, its continuous role as a seat of power, and its multiple cultural layers. The World Heritage Site comprises two sections: the archaeological site at 18 Hoang Dieu Street and the central axis of the Nguyen Dynasty’s Citadel of Hanoi, which together create an integrated heritage complex. This was the most important sector of Thang Long Citadel, the capital of Dai Viet under the Ly, Tran, and Le dynasties from the 11th to the 18th centuries. It was also the core of the earlier Dai
